name: Allied Cash Advance
description_short: At 16165 N 83rd Ave Ste 243 in Peoria, AZ 85382 (Center at Arrowhead), Allied Cash Advance can be reached at +1 480-691-2997.
description_long:
Allied Cash Advance is a storefront lender operating locations across the United States, including a Mesa, Arizona location at 2706 East University Drive. The company specializes in small-to-medium personal loans designed for consumers who need fast access to cash for unexpected expenses or bill consolidation. Their business model centers on rapid underwriting and funding, with same-day disbursement available for in-store applicants using debit cards and next-day funding for online applicants.

The company offers installment loans (unsecured personal loans with fixed repayment schedules), Netspend Prepaid Mastercard accounts, and refinancing/reloading options for existing customers. Applications can be completed online, in-store, or by phone (where available). The underwriting process evaluates income, bank account activity, and government-issued ID, with explicit flexibility on credit history—stating they cannot determine whether bad or no credit will prevent approval. Loan amounts and terms vary based on individual underwriting decisions.

Allied Cash Advance distinguishes itself through accessibility to consumers with poor or no credit history, same-day funding via debit card for in-store applicants, and an established retail network with extended hours (10 AM–6 PM weekdays, 10 AM–2 PM Saturday). The Mesa location maintains a 4.9-star Google rating across 379 reviews. The company positions installment loans as an alternative to payday loans, emphasizing fixed repayment schedules over the single balloon-payment structure of traditional payday advances.

However, the company does not disclose APR, fees, or maximum loan amounts on their website, and they cannot advise applicants on credit score impact. As a non-bank lender offering small loans at quick speed, rates are likely to be substantially higher than traditional banks. The lack of transparent pricing and the nature of emergency cash products suggest this is a high-cost credit option suited only for genuine emergencies where speed is critical.
address: Center at Arrowhead, Peoria Center at Arrowhead, 16165 N 83rd Ave Ste 243, Peoria, AZ 85382

company_info.headquarters: Center at Arrowhead, Peoria Center at Arrowhead, 16165 N 83rd Ave Ste 243, Peoria, AZ 85382

pros: Same-day funding available via debit card for in-store applicants; next-day for online
No explicit credit score requirement; will consider applicants with bad or no credit
Fixed installment repayment schedule (not balloon-payment payday structure)
Multiple application channels: online, in-store, and phone
Extended hours including Saturday (10 AM–2 PM) for convenience
Fast approval process (typically 30 minutes with debit card at location)
4.9-star Google rating based on 379 customer reviews
cons: No APR, fee structure, or loan limit transparency disclosed on website
Cannot advise on credit score impact; applicant responsibility to assess damage
Requires active checking account (minimum 30 days old) plus proof of income
Requires vehicle ownership proof in Arizona (additional barrier)
High-cost lending product; rates likely substantially above traditional bank loans
best_for: Consumers with poor or no credit who need emergency cash within 24 hours
Unbanked or underbanked individuals with valid checking accounts
Borrowers needing installment structure rather than lump-sum payday loans
diagnosis: Allied Cash Advance is best for consumers facing genuine financial emergencies who cannot access traditional credit and need cash within hours. Critical caveat: this is high-cost credit with non-transparent pricing; applicants must obtain full APR and fee details before committing and should only use as a last resort for true emergencies.
services: Unsecured installment loans with fixed repayment schedules
Retail refinancing and reloading for existing customers
In-store customer service and loan counseling
